hypothesis: Systems-optimizer recovery and reporting become more deterministic if terminal recency must resolve to exactly one latest retained learning entry per status, so tied kept or rejected timestamps cannot leave `last_kept_change` or `last_rejected_change` ambiguous.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `findLatestRetainedLearningEntries` could return multiple kept or rejected ids that shared the newest `learning_log.recorded_at`, and `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` still accepted `state.last_kept_change` or `state.last_rejected_change` as long as each pointer matched any one of those tied ids. That left terminal recency ambiguous under same-second retained timestamps. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any kept or rejected status whose latest retained `learning_log.recorded_at` is shared by multiple ids, and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails tied latest kept or rejected `learning_log.recorded_at` values and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Terminal systems-optimizer recency needs a singleton latest retained `learning_log.recorded_at` per status; if multiple ratchets share the newest second, terminal pointers stop being deterministic.
